content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
1 Practices Commission
February 3,
1994
Lisa Hirsh
670 Windsor Drive
Benicia,
CA 94510
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-93-449
Dear Ms. Hirsh:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your responsibilities as a member of the Benicia Unified
School District Board under the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) 1
Specifically,
you asked
whether the Benicia Unified School District’s Bond Update,
a flyer
which contained a copy of your signature and was distributed
around the community in October 1993, violated the mass mailing
laws of the Act.
Because your request seeks general guidance
about the Benicia Unified School Board’s course of conduct in
connection with the preparation and distribution of what appears
to be a mass mailing
1
we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.
In your letter,
you stated that during the election period
the district staff, at the direction of the governing board,
prepared a flyer (the “Bond Update”)
to be distributed to the
public.
You further stated that it is both the board’s written
policy and the district’s usual practice to send board
communications over the signature of the board president and that,
accordingly, staff affixed a copy of your signature and
distributed the “Bond Update” in various ways around the
community.
Though you did not participate in the actual
preparation of the flyer or personally sign it,
you acknowledged
in your letter that staff showed the flyer to you before it went
out in October 1993.
You were up for reelection in November 1993.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322.5660


Our File No.
1-93-449
Page 2
•
Since your question refers to conduct that has already
occurred,
we cannot provide you with advice concerning that
conduct.
Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A) provides that formal written
advice must be declined where the “requestor is seeking advice
relating to past conduct.”
If you should require our guidance in the future regarding
whether a mass mailing is either permissible or prohibited under
the Act,
we would be pleased to assist you so long as your
questions are prospective in nature.
In this regard,
I am
enclosing a copy of the law regulating mass mailings
(Regulation
18901)
and a memorandum explaining the general rules of mass
mailings.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—59O1.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel
Enclosures
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 1O per page.

Because your request seeks general guidance about the Benicia Unified School Board’s course of conduct in connection with the preparation and distribution of what appears to be a mass mailing 1 we are treating your request as one for informal assistance. In your letter, you stated that during the election period the district staff, at the direction of the governing board, prepared a flyer (the “Bond Update”) to be distributed to the public. You further stated that it is both the board’s written policy and the district’s usual practice to send board communications over the signature of the board president and that, accordingly, staff affixed a copy of your signature and distributed the “Bond Update” in various ways around the community. Though you did not participate in the actual preparation of the flyer or personally sign it, you acknowledged in your letter that staff showed the flyer to you before it went out in October 1993. You were up for reelection in November 1993. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322.5660 Our File No. 1-93-449 Page 2 • Since your question refers to conduct that has already occurred, we cannot provide you with advice concerning that conduct. Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A) provides that formal written advice must be declined where the “requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct.” If you should require our guidance in the future regarding whether a mass mailing is either permissible or prohibited under the Act, we would be pleased to assist you so long as your questions are prospective in nature. In this regard, I am enclosing a copy of the law regulating mass mailings (Regulation 18901) and a memorandum explaining the general rules of mass mailings. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322—59O1. Sincerely, Steven G.
